name: Chrome
description: Web browser from Google built on the Chromium engine.

name: Discord
description: Hosted voice, video, and text chat for communities and friend groups.

name: Dropbox
description: Hosted file sync and sharing across devices with team folders.

name: Figma
description: Collaborative interface design tool for vector UI work and prototyping.

name: Gmail
description: Hosted email service from Google with built-in spam filtering and search.

name: Google Analytics
description: Web analytics service that tracks visitor behavior, conversions, and traffic sources.

name: Mailchimp
description: Hosted newsletter and email marketing service with templates and audience tracking.

name: Microsoft Word
description: Word processor for documents, letters, and reports with rich formatting.

name: Notion
description: All-in-one workspace for notes, docs, wikis, and lightweight project management.

name: Photoshop
description: Raster image editor for photo retouching, compositing, and digital painting.

name: Slack
description: Channel-based team messaging with threads, integrations, and search.

name: Zoom
description: Hosted video conferencing service for meetings, webinars, and screen sharing.

name: AbiWord
description: Lightweight word processor that opens DOC, DOCX, RTF, and plain text.

name: AFFiNE
description: Knowledge base that blends pages, whiteboards, and tables in one canvas.

name: Akira
description: Native Linux design tool focused on UI mockups and modern interface work.

name: Anytype
description: Local-first, encrypted personal knowledge base with object-based notes.

name: AppFlowy
description: Open-source workspace for notes, wikis, and projects with offline-first sync.

name: BigBlueButton
description: Video conferencing built for online classrooms with whiteboards and breakout rooms.

name: Brave
description: Chromium-based browser with built-in ad and tracker blocking by default.

name: Calligra Words
description: KDE's word processor with frame-based layout for documents and reports.

name: CryptPad
description: End-to-end encrypted suite for documents, sheets, slides, and shared whiteboards.

name: CryptPad
description: End-to-end encrypted suite for documents, sheets, slides, and shared whiteboards.

name: darktable
description: Non-destructive raw photo workflow tool, like a free Lightroom replacement.

name: Element
description: Encrypted Matrix client for chat, voice, and video across federated servers.

name: Element
description: Encrypted Matrix client for chat, voice, and video across federated servers.

name: Etherpad
description: Real-time collaborative text editing in the browser, with no account needed.

name: Excalidraw
description: Whiteboard for hand-drawn-style diagrams, wireframes, and quick UI sketches.

name: File Browser
description: Web-based file manager you point at a directory on your own server.

name: Firefox
description: Independent web browser from Mozilla with strong tracking protections.

name: GIMP
description: Long-standing raster image editor with layers, masks, and a deep plugin ecosystem.

name: GoatCounter
description: Tiny, no-cookie web stats designed to be readable on a single screen.

name: Inkscape
description: Vector graphics editor for illustrations, icons, and scalable artwork.

name: Jami
description: Peer-to-peer video, voice, and text with no central server and no account.

name: Jami
description: Peer-to-peer video, voice, and text with no central server and no account.

name: Jitsi Meet
description: Browser-based video conferencing with no account required to start a call.

name: Joplin
description: Markdown notes and to-dos with end-to-end encryption and your-choice sync backends.

name: Keila
description: Open-source newsletter tool that connects to your own SMTP for sending.

name: Krita
description: Painting app with brush engines tuned for illustrators and concept artists.

name: LibreOffice Writer
description: Mature desktop word processor with full ODF support and DOCX compatibility.

name: LibreWolf
description: A Firefox fork preconfigured for privacy with telemetry and pocket removed.

name: Listmonk
description: Self-hosted newsletter and mailing-list manager with a fast Go backend.

name: LiveKit Meet
description: Demo conferencing app on top of the LiveKit WebRTC stack you can host yourself.

name: Logseq
description: Outliner for networked notes that stores everything as plain text on disk.

name: Matomo
description: Full-featured analytics with on-prem hosting, opt-out, and GDPR-friendly defaults.

name: Mattermost
description: Self-hosted team messaging with channels, threads, and integrations.

name: Mautic
description: Marketing automation with email campaigns, segmentation, and lead scoring.

name: Movim
description: XMPP-based social and chat platform for federated communities, web-first.

name: Nextcloud
description: Self-hosted file sync, sharing, and collaboration with calendar, mail, and apps.

name: ONLYOFFICE
description: Document, spreadsheet, and presentation editors with strong DOCX fidelity.

name: PairDrop
description: Browser-based AirDrop-style file transfer between devices on the same network.

name: Penpot
description: Browser-based design and prototyping tool built on web standards like SVG.

name: Plausible
description: Cookie-free, privacy-friendly web analytics that fits on a single dashboard.

name: Postal
description: Self-hosted mail server you can drive newsletter sends through, with a clean API.

name: Proton Mail
description: End-to-end encrypted email hosted in Switzerland, with open-source clients.

name: Revolt
description: Discord-like chat for communities, with self-hosting for full data control.

name: Rocket.Chat
description: Self-hosted team chat with channels, video calls, and federation support.

name: Seafile
description: File sync server with library-based storage and optional client-side encryption.

name: SilverBullet
description: Self-hosted, extensible Markdown notebook that runs in any browser.

name: SimpleLogin
description: Email aliasing service, useful for keeping your real address out of sign-up forms.

name: Standard Notes
description: Encrypted notes app with a simple format and long-term durability as goals.

name: Syncthing
description: Continuous peer-to-peer file sync between your own devices, with no cloud.

name: Thunderbird
description: Mozilla's desktop email client with calendar, address book, and IMAP support.

name: Tuta Mail
description: Encrypted email and calendar with open-source apps and zero-knowledge storage.

name: Umami
description: Lightweight, self-hosted web analytics with no cookies and tidy dashboards.

name: Ungoogled Chromium
description: Chromium with Google-specific features and dependencies stripped out.

name: Zulip
description: Threaded team chat organized by topic so conversations stay readable later.
